data.title: Security First Insurance Deploys Guidewire InsuranceNow to Modernize Operations
data.description: Security First Insurance and Guidewire announced that Security First deployed Guidewire InsuranceNow to modernize its operations to enhance agent and customer service. The company implemented InsuranceNow to its homeowners and dwelling fire lines of business simultaneously.

data.title: Tranche Update on InterContinental Hotels Group PLC's Equity Buyback Plan announced on February 18, 2025.
data.description: From July 1, 2025 to October 23, 2025, the company has repurchased 2,303,359 shares, representing 1.47% for $275 million. With this, the company has completed the repurchase of 6,103,359 shares, representing 3.89% for $700 million under the buyback announced on February 18, 2025.

Encompass Health Declares Quarterly Cash Dividend on Common Stock, Payable on January 15, 2026
data.description: Encompass Health Corp. announced that its board of directors has declared a quarterly cash dividend on its common stock of $0.19 per share, payable on January 15, 2026, to holders of record on January 2, 2026.

data.title: Hexaware Appoints Eravi Gopan as President and Global Head ? High Tech, Products, and Platforms
data.description:
Hexaware Technologies announced the appointment of Eravi Gopan as President and Global Head ? High Tech, Products, and Platforms. He will lead Hexaware's global strategy, growth, and client success initiatives across the vertical. With deep experience in SaaS, AI, and technology services, Eravi is recognized for building and scaling businesses across geographies and industry verticals.

He has led high-growth teams in venture-funded Silicon Valley start-ups, large global enterprises, and partnered deeply with foundational technology companies. He served as Chief Revenue Officer at a Google Ventures-backed SaaS platform, where he led a successful transformation and acquisition, and more recently as the first Chief Commercial Officer of an Accel Ventures-backed enterprise AI startup. A mentor at the Founder Institute, Silicon Valley and an advisor to multiple startups, Eravi previously spent a long tenure at Wipro, where he built several first-time businesses across diverse industry verticals.

He was part of the senior leadership team that scaled Wipro's global technology business and, in his last role, served as Global Business Head for Platforms and Software Products. Eravi holds degrees in electronics engineering and business management and lives in the San Francisco Bay Area.

First Citizens BancShares, Inc. Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter Ended September 30, 2025
data.description: First Citizens BancShares, Inc. reported earnings results for the third quarter ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported net interest income was USD 1,734 million compared to USD 1,796 million a year ago. Net income was USD 568 million compared to USD 639 million a year ago.

S&P cuts India's Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles outlook on slow Jaguar Land Rover recovery
data.description:
(Reuters) -Ratings agency S&P Global said on Thursday it is downgrading the outlook of India's Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles to 'negative' on "slow recovery" of its UK unit, Jaguar Land Rover, which was hit by a cyber attack earlier this year.

(Reporting by Hritam Mukherjee in Bengaluru; Editing by Shailesh Kuber)

HSBC Puts Egypt Retail Business Under Review Amid Global Overhaul
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud


HSBC Holdings plans to review strategic options for its retail banking business in Egypt, as the lender moves to scale back some operations as part of an overhaul of its global footprint.

Europe's biggest bank by assets has been seeking to streamline operations and cut costs since Chief Executive Georges Elhedery took the helm last year.

The U.K.-based, Asia-focused lender said Thursday that the review of its Egyptian business would center on its retail banking operations only, leaving out wholesale banking activities.

The move follows similar strategic reviews the group opened earlier this year for its retail businesses in Australia, Indonesia and Sri Lanka. Elhedery said in an earnings call in July that another review, in Bangladesh, had concluded and would result in the wind-down of its retail business there in the second half of the year.


Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-23-25 1107ET

IBM Shares Down as 3Q Software Revenue Growth Disappoints
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese


International Business Machines shares fell after disappointing third-quarter performance in two of its most critical business segments, Red Hat and transaction-processing platforms.

Shares fell 4% to $275.82, a recovery from lows of $263.56 closer to the open.

IBM late Wednesday reported about a 10% rise in third-quarter software revenue to $7.21 billion, in line with Wall Street expectations. The Armonk, N.Y., technology company's hybrid-cloud revenue, which includes Red Hat, rose in the quarter but at a slower clip than in the previous quarter, while TPP revenue fell 1%, compared with a 1% uptick in the second quarter.

Melius analyst Ben Reitzes noted in a report that these segments are a big focus for investors. Red Hat and TPP form the backbone of IBM's strategy to bridge traditional enterprise information technology with modern cloud innovation, he said.

"We understand there will be frustration here in Software since Red Hat tends to support the stock and the miss came as a surprise," Reitzes said.

Despite this, the analyst thinks the segments are still in good shape. "Profit metrics are better, Red Hat and [artificial intelligence] bookings are solid and TPP can pick up, making the case for improvement compelling," Reitzes added, pointing out that Red Hat growth in bookings is still trending above revenue at 20%.

Total revenue for the quarter rose 9.1% to $16.33 billion, ahead of the $16.09 billion analysts expected, according to FactSet. The company also logged a profit of $1.74 billion, or $1.84 a share, in the quarter ended Sept. 30, from a loss of $330 million, or 36 cents a share, a year earlier.


Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-23-25 1043ET

DeepSeek More Affordable For Africa's Tech Startups
data.description:
Chinese firms are making headway in the AI battle for Africa, with startups increasingly choosing cheaper AI models such as DeepSeek over western competitors including OpenAI. Bloomberg's Senior Africa Reporter Loni Prinsloo joins Lizzy Burden on "Daybreak Europe" to discuss why founders in Africa are brushing off concerns about dependence on China and data privacy.
